ops.title: v0.13.0 grep guard judges each grep statement per-occurrence with a no-stat data-ext escape; -o forfeits the stat lane (oHnb 57× amplification); sink greps with paths must qualify; the design's accepted parity holes and the jj describe-after-push divergen
ops.body:
cc-context v0.13.0 (def3c843, pack 0.4.0) rewrote `_bounded_file_grep` in plugin/hooks/search_guards.py after the guard falsely blocked `cd … && gog … > x.json; grep -oiE '…' x.json | head`. Design decisions a future "fix" must not undo:

- **Per-occurrence, not whole-line**: `GrepFlood` walks every grep in `cl.parts` (sink flag = previous part's op is `|`); the old whole-line `is_single_command` gate made the passthrough unreachable in any compound. The `_unpiped` early-return is KEPT deliberately — a pure-pipe line with no unpiped grep (e.g. `cat x | grep . huge.py`) stays out of scope, matching main-before-fix and rg (accepted blind spot, future work).
- **Data-ext escape stats nothing** by design — that no-stat property is what lets redirect-created and cd-relative files pass (PreToolUse runs before execution; there is no cd-tracking). Don't bolt a stat or size cap onto it; it is exact rg parity (rg's escape is size-exempt and FIFO-blind too).
- **`-o` is lexable but forfeits the stat lane**: codex refuted the "~2× bound" — `grep -oHnb` emits per-match filename/line/byte prefixes, 57× on a real file. `-o` on data-ext targets is fine (rg parity).
- **GREP_OPTIONS env forfeits everything** (macOS grep honors it; CommandLine.parse strips assignments into `cmd.env`/`env_dict`, so injected `-r` never reaches args); any env with path operands forfeits, but `… | LC_ALL=C grep pat` (no paths) stays a valid sink filter.
- **Sink greps with path operands must qualify** (`bounded | grep -r . /` ignores stdin and searches files); unparseable or operand-less sink greps keep the stdin-filter presumption.
- Rejected as parity/out-of-scope: FIFO-named data files, `find -exec`/`xargs` grep indirection, glob-of-data-files operands.

**Why (process)**: two codex passes (finder + refuter) on the first implementation found 4 real wrong-allow holes the tests missed — adversarial review of guard changes pays. codex-wrapper agents stalled twice waiting on their own background monitors; the run that worked used `--sandbox read-only` + static reasoning (codex's own shell experiments tripped its content filter mid-run).

**How to apply (jj trap)**: `jj describe` after `jj commit` REWRITES the commit id; `jj bookmark move --to <old-id>` then creates a divergent change, and pushing the old id drops the amendment. Describe before pushing and move bookmarks to the post-describe id — or put trailers in the original `jj commit -m`. Related: [[jj-shared-workingcopy-collision]] (this fix rode in a `jj workspace add -r main` workspace; `uvx capt-hook --hooks <dir> test` works there but cc-notes-pack nudges return None without .git, and the LLM-judge gates run stubbed — expect ~6 environmental failures that CI won't have).
